nodes.label: AI as literature discovery tool
nodes.grounding_span: We used AI to help us find more articles in the literature

nodes.label: AI as explanation aid for difficult topics
nodes.grounding_span: We also used it to describe difficult topics or to summarize papers

nodes.label: AI accuracy and trustworthiness
nodes.grounding_span: I would want AI to be 100% accurate so that it is completely trustable as a resource

nodes.label: AI as fallback when traditional methods fail
nodes.label_negative: AI as primary or independent tool
nodes.grounding_span: We would usually go to AI if Google or PubMed was not able to find some good sources

nodes.label: AI for tasks with low difficulty
nodes.label_negative: AI for tasks requiring high specificity or collaboration
nodes.grounding_span: it's just not that difficult to format a paper to a journal's specifications

nodes.label: Research efficiency and thoroughness
nodes.grounding_span: find more articles in the literature

nodes.label: Epistemic certainty and trust
nodes.grounding_span: I would want AI to be 100% accurate so that it is completely trustable

nodes.label: Methodological autonomy and control
nodes.grounding_span: we made sure to not only trust the AI to do the thinking for us

nodes.label: Pragmatic acceptance of AI as supplementary tool
nodes.grounding_span: We used AI to help us find more articles in the literature

nodes.label: Skepticism toward AI due to accuracy concerns
nodes.grounding_span: I don't think I can always trust what the AI says

nodes.label: Conditional openness to future AI use in outlining
nodes.grounding_span: I think we definitely might use AI in the future perhaps to propose an outline

nodes.label: Systematic verification through source checking
nodes.grounding_span: We ask the AI specifically for journal articles that we then read through to see if what it is saying makes sense

nodes.label: Pragmatic task prioritization — use AI only when traditional methods are insufficient
nodes.grounding_span: We would usually go to AI if Google or PubMed was not able to find some good sources
